WORLDSCOPE/Sections/Ukrainian Internal/2026-05-31

Ukrainian Internal · 2026-05-31

Ukrainian press: national + Kyiv-local + government · 120 records across 11 sources

Kyiv Post · 82 records

8 Million Ukrainians Abroad Pose Security Challenge, Sybiha Says — Roughly 8 million Ukrainians are currently living outside the country, posing a long-term security and demographic challenge, Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said. He said Uk

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

raine is developing policies to encourage citizens to return home once security conditions improve, while acknowledging that some host countries benefit from Ukrainians’ skills and economic contributions and may not want them to leave.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Champions League Final Under the Shadow of the War in Ukraine — The Champions League final highlights how the war in Ukraine can permeate European football in subtle ways. Russian goalkeeper Matvei Safonov’s silent presence, Georgian star K

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

hvicha Kvaratskhelia’s ties to Russia and Europe, and Ukrainian defender Illia Zabarnyi’s clear stance against Russian aggression all show contrasting positions that making neutrality in European football more an aspiration than a tangible condition.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Checking Your Digital Locks: Practical Ways to Secure Your Daily Connections in Ukraine — Relying on public hotspots in urban centers requires a proactive approach to data security. Read more here to learn how to secure your network.

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent
country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Crimea Imposes Strict Gasoline Rationing as Land Corridor Collapse Sparks Fuel Crisis — The Russian occupation administration in Crimea has introduced strict fuel rationing across the peninsula, capping the sale of A-95 gasoline at 20 liter

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

s per day per person starting Saturday, May 30. The regional decree, announced by the Russian-installed head Sergei Aksyonov, mimics near-identical restrictions enforced earlier in occupied Sevastopol to curb widespread public panic.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Drone Sighting Forces Hour-Long Ground Stop at Munich Airport — Munich Airport completely halted all flight operations for over an hour on Saturday morning, May 30, following a suspected drone intrusion near the airfield. The security shutd

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

own began around 9:00 a.m. local time after multiple pilots reported seeing a suspicious aerial object close to the facility, prompting Federal Police to completely close both runways.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Estonia Deploys First Border Anti-Drone Monitoring Systems — Estonia has installed and activated its first stationary drone detection and monitoring systems across three key land border sectors in the southeastern region. Estonian Interior

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

Minister Igor Taro confirmed that the deployment marks the beginning of a larger strategic initiative to build a comprehensive, nationwide anti-drone radar shield. To cover areas currently lacking stationary sensors, the Estonian Police and Border Guard Board

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

EU to Unlock €16.4 Billion in Frozen Funds for Hungary Following Reforms — European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen confirmed that the EU will unlock €16.4 billion of previously frozen recovery and cohesion funds for Hungary follo

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

wing successful policy reforms under the country’s new government. The financial package includes €10 billion from the Next Generation EU recovery fund, €4.2 billion in cohesion funds, and an additional €2.2 billion tied to the completion of academic freedom b

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Explosions Heard in Kyiv as Russia Launches Evening Missile Attack on Ukraine — Ukrainian authorities warned of an incoming Russian missile attack on Friday evening after cruise missiles were detected heading toward western regions of the c

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ountry. Explosions were reported in Kyiv and the Kyiv region as air defenses responded.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Explosions Hit Russian City Home to Shahed Drone, Iskander Missile Factory — Residents of the Russian city of Cheboksary reported explosions and air raid sirens on Friday, while local authorities denied any incident. The city hosts a key mi

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

litary electronics manufacturer supplying components for some of Russia’s most widely used strike systems.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Fewer US Weapons for Nato: Can Europe Defend Itself? — European commentators warn that reported US plans to sharply reduce NATO military support could leave Europe dangerously exposed, especially on the alliance’s eastern flank. Analysts sa

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

y Europe must rapidly strengthen its own defence capabilities, while debates intensify over Germany’s military role, EU political integration, and the risks posed by Russia and China exploiting Western uncertainty.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Heir to Iranian Throne: ‘We’re in This Fight Together, Iranians Are Suffering Under a Regime That Supports Russia's Aggression Against Ukraine’ — Prince Reza Pahlavi, heir to the Iranian throne, shared with Kyiv Post his view of the common

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

destiny of Ukrainians and Iranians and expressed gratitude to all Ukrainians who are fighting for Ukraine’s sovereignty and supporting Iranians in their protests against the Islamist regime.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

How Сan NATO Respond to Romania Drone Crash? — NATO condemned Russia after a drone struck a residential building in Romania, injuring two civilians. Alliance officials say the focus is likely to remain on strengthening eastern flank defense

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

s, improving drone interception capabilities, and potentially using Article 4 consultations rather than invoking the collective defense clause under Article 5.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Iranian Missile Strike on US Base in Kuwait Wounds 5 and Destroys MQ-9 Reapers — Iran launched a short-range ballistic missile targeting the US Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait over the past 24 hours, directly threatening a pending peace agr

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

eement. Kuwaiti air defense systems intercepted the Iranian Fateh-110 missile, but falling debris wounded approximately five US service members and civilian contractors, while completely destroying one MQ-9 Reaper drone and severely damaging another.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Japan Contributes $14.7M to NATO’s PURL Initiative for Ukraine — Japan has added $14.7 million to NATO’s PURL initiative to fund non-lethal equipment for Ukraine. President Volodymyr Zelensky thanked Tokyo and said PURL contributions have r

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

eached about $1.5 billion this year, with 28 countries participating so far.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Kellogg Plans Visit to Kyiv Amid Ongoing Push for Ukraine Peace Deal — Former US presidential envoy for Ukraine, Keith Kellogg, has announced plans to visit Kyiv at the end of summer. His visit is expected to focus on security guarantees fo

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

r Ukraine and options for a negotiated end to Russia’s full-scale invasion. Kellogg, who has previously supported a 28-point peace framework and criticized NATO’s cautious approach, presents the trip as part of efforts to secure a realistic but durable settlem

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Kyiv Briefly Under Air Alert Over Ballistic Missile Threat — Kyiv and several Ukrainian regions were briefly placed under air raid alerts in the early hours of Friday after the Air Force warned of a ballistic missile threat to eastern, nort

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

hern and central regions. The warning came amid repeated Russian missile threats, including from MiG-31K aircraft capable of carrying Kinzhal ballistic missiles.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Medvedev Tells EU to ‘Shut Up’ After Romanian Drone Strike — Former Russian President Dmitry Medvedev, one of the Kremlin’s most outspoken hardliners and currently deputy chairman of Russia’s Security Council, dismissed European criticism a

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

fter a Russian drone hit a residential building in Romania. In a profanity-laced Telegram post, Medvedev mocked EU leaders, warned that similar incidents could happen again and told European countries to “shut up” following condemnation of the strike by NATO a

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Morning in Ukraine — The economy plays a pivotal role in a nation’s security, prosperity, growth, and just about everything else that matters. It is an aggregate of capital, labor, technologies, innovations, human capital, and ecology. If p

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

roperly planned and managed, it will signal the dawn of a beautiful new “morning” in Ukraine. In the hands of an incompetent or uninterested political and economic faux “elite,” they will create chaos and waste.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Moscow Erects Third Air Defense Ring as Pantsir Systems Placed on Civilian Rooftops — Russia has begun forming its third and largest outer air defense ring encompassing Moscow and surrounding suburbs to shield the capital from continuous Uk

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

rainian deep-strike drone operations. As part of an inner defense layer focused tightly around the Kremlin, Russian forces used heavy Mi-26T transport helicopters to hoist a new Pantsir-SMD-E air defense system onto the roof of the Nordstar Tower business cent

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

NATO Condemns Russia After Drone Explodes at Romanian Apartment Block — NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte sharply criticized Russia’s “reckless” behavior and pledged to defend every inch of alliance territory after a Russian strike drone en

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

tered Romanian airspace and exploded against a 10-story apartment block in Galați. The unprecedented impact tore through a top-floor flat, wounding a woman and a child.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

NATO Military Chief Reassures Washington on 5% GDP Spending Goal — The Chairman of the NATO Military Committee, Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, stated that European allies are systematically expanding their military capabilities to meet an u

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

nprecedented defense spending target of 5% of individual member GDP. The Italian admiral’s remarks followed a blistering speech delivered by US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, where the Pentagon chief heavily criticized

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Nawrocki Wants Zelensky Stripped of Poland’s Highest Honor — Polish President Karol Nawrocki said he will ask Poland’s Order of the White Eagle chapter to consider revoking the country’s highest state honor from President Volodymyr Zelensky

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

. The proposal follows Ukraine’s decision to name a military unit after the Ukrainian Insurgent Army (UPA), a move that reignited longstanding disagreements between Poland and Ukraine over the legacy of WWII-era nationalist movements.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

One Dead in Chernihiv Region Following Massive Russian Drone Assault — Early Sunday morning, Russian forces executed a massive aerial assault against Ukraine, deploying 229 combat and decoy drones from multiple directions, including Oryol,

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

Kursk, and occupied Crimea. Ukrainian air defense forces successfully shot down or electronically suppressed 212 of the incoming UAVs across northern and eastern regions. Despite the high interception rate, 14 strike drones hit 11 locations, including a fatal

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Pentagon Promises US Will ‘Find a Way’ to Sustain Help for Ukraine’s Defense — US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth strongly emphasized that the United States will find a concrete way to ensure Ukraine can continue defending itself against for

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

eign aggression. While praising Ukraine’s high combat efficiency, Hegseth defended the administration’s strategy of successfully pushing European allies to step up their financial and material support.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Pentagon Ready to Resume Strikes if Iran Peace Deal Collapses — US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced that the US maintains widespread military readiness to instantly resume air and naval strikes against Iran if diplomatic efforts to

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

finalize a peace agreement collapse. Speaking at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, Hegseth emphasized that the US has positioned vast strategic reserves globally and regionally to enforce Washington’s prerequisites

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Poland Becomes First Nation to Receive SAFE Loan Funding for Defense Procurement — Poland on Friday became the first country to receive its initial payout from the European Commission’s €150 billion Security Action For Europe (SAFE) loan pr

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ogram, securing €6.6 billion of its €43.7 billion total defense allocation. The funding release comes amid a massive domestic military acquisition drive, with Prime Minister Donald Tusk announcing a total of 40 active contracts valued at 100 billion zlotys (€2

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Poroshenko ‘Very Surprised’ in Meeting With Zelensky Over War Ending Terms — Petro Poroshenko said his meeting with President Volodymyr Zelensky on Wednesday focused on ending the war and achieving peace. The talks were part of Zelensky’s s

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

eparate meetings with parliamentary faction leaders, amid long-standing political rivalry between the two figures.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Poroshenko: Now Is the Time to Force Putin Into Peace — During the Black Sea Security Forum, Ukraine’s fifth president Petro Poroshenko spoke about the risks Ukraine faced in 2014 and the situation now which – in his view – is significantly

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

better and could create an opportunity to force Russia into a peace agreement on Ukraine’s terms.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Putin Claims War ‘Nearing Its End’ Based on Supposed Russian Advances — Speaking to state outlets on Friday, Russian President Vladimir Putin asserted that his analysis of the current battlefield situation indicates the war against Ukraine

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

is approaching its final phase. Putin claimed that Russian forces are advancing across all operational directions, though he acknowledged that establishing a definitive timeline for the conclusion of hostilities remains impossible under active combat condition

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Putin Leaves Kazakhstan With Empty Rhetoric and Panic Over Armenia, Goble Says — In an interview with Kyiv Post, Paul Goble said Putin’s Kazakhstan visit produced symbolic language but few substantive gains, as Astana resisted Moscow’s pres

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

sure on trade routes and migrant labor. Goble said Putin’s lengthy focus on Armenia revealed Kremlin panic over Yerevan’s westward shift, ahead of the June 7 election.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Romania to Close Russian Consulate After Drone Strike — Romania will expel Russia’s consul general in Constanța and close its consulate after a Russian drone struck a residential building in Galați on Friday, President Nicușor Dan said. The

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

attack, which injured two people, was described as the most serious security incident on Romanian territory since the start of Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Romania, Ukraine to Boost Joint Drone Production After Russian Drone Strike — Romania and Ukraine will accelerate cooperation on jointly produced drones, Romanian President Nicușor Dan said after speaking with President Volodymyr Zelensky f

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ollowing a Russian drone strike on a residential building in the Romanian city of Galati.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russian Double-Tap Strike on Zaporizhzhia Industrial Zone Kills 1, Wounds 3 — Russian forces executed a deadly “double-tap” drone assault against the industrial infrastructure of Zaporizhzhia on Saturday. Following an initial early morning

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

strike that left a 40-year-old worker critically injured, Russian forces launched a repeat strike on the same facility, igniting a massive fire and damaging nearby residential buildings. One of the wounded victims later died in the hospital from severe trauma.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russian Drone Attack Injures Two in Poltava Region — Russian drone strikes hit several locations in the Poltava district on Friday evening, damaging private homes, an outpatient clinic, household buildings, cars and power lines. Regional of

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ficials said two people were injured and received medical assistance at the scene, while emergency crews extinguished fires caused by the attack.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russian Drone Hits Apartment Building in NATO Member Romania, Injuring Two — Romania’s defense ministry said a Russian drone entered Romanian airspace during Moscow’s overnight attacks on Ukraine and crashed onto the roof of an apartment bu

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ilding in Galați. The impact triggered a fire, and emergency services said two people were wounded.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russian Drone in Romania Was a Deliberate Provocation, Analyst Says — Foreign policy analyst Paul Goble says Russia’s drone incursion into Romania was a deliberate provocation designed to test NATO unity and pressure Moldova. He argued that

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

Putin’s denial was predictable, as Moscow benefits when allies debate responsibility instead of responding collectively.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russian Drone Strike Hits Cargo Ship En Route From Odesa Region To Turkey, Ukraine Says — A cargo ship ANT sailing from Ukraine’s Odesa region to Turkey was struck Friday morning by a drone in the Black Sea, causing a fire and injuring two

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

crew members, Ukraine’s Navy said. The vessel was later evacuated and the blaze extinguished. Kyiv attributed the attack to Russian forces, warning of continued risks to civilian shipping.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russian Strikes Hit Industrial Facility in Zaporizhzhia and Civilian Sectors in Kherson — Russian forces carried out fresh targeted strikes the night of Friday-Saturday, hitting an industrial infrastructure facility in the city of Zaporizhz

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

hia. Regional Military Administration Head Ivan Fedorov confirmed that the heavy morning strike critically injured a 40-year-old man. The industrial attack followed a late-night Russian loitering munition strike on the Korabelnyi district of Kherson, which wou

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russia’s Oreshnik Missile Built 9 Years Ago with Local Parts — Ukrainian missile forensics experts have debunked key Kremlin claims regarding the “Oreshnik” intermediate-range ballistic missile, revealing that the weapon is nearly a decade

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

old and contains exclusively Russian and Belarusian components. Presenting recovered electronic circuitry from a January 2026 strike on a gas depot in the Lviv region, analysts demonstrated that the missile was actually assembled in 2017 using components dated

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russia’s War Costs Could Exceed Budget by $28 Billion — Russia’s war spending could surpass planned budget levels by 2 trillion rubles ($28 billion) this year, according to a report citing a letter from Finance Minister Anton Siluanov. The

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

document reportedly warned of growing fiscal pressures and urged spending freezes in other sectors to offset rising military costs. The report highlights mounting strains on Russia’s finances due to record defense spending.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

SBU, HUR and Navy Foil FSB-Linked Plot to Smuggle Armed Drone Into Odesa via Sea Route — Ukraine’s SBU, HUR, and Navy have dismantled a Black Sea smuggling route used to transport weapons and Russian-made drones under the cover of cigarette

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

shipments from occupied Abkhazia. Four suspects were detained during a year-long operation that also uncovered an FSB-linked plot to deliver an armed UAV to Odesa.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Sky-High War Overheads Stagnate Russia’s Civilian Sectors — The staggering costs of Russia’s war against Ukraine have fractured the country’s domestic market into a distinct “dual economy,” characterized by an overheated military-industrial

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

complex and severe stagnation across civilian sectors. Skyrocketing capital, labor, and product costs – compounded by aggressive tax hikes and increased internal borrowing – have paralyzed non-military industries as the initial boost from state defense spendi

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Trump Details Ultimatum for Reopening Strait of Hormuz in Iran Deal — US President Donald Trump announced that a multi-lateral framework to extend the fragile Persian Gulf ceasefire by 60 days has been largely negotiated, but affirmed he is

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

reviewing final terms before signing. Under the proposed memorandum of understanding, Iran must commit to never acquiring nuclear weapons, dismantle its maritime mine networks within 30 days, and allow unrestricted traffic through the Strait of Hormuz withou

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Tsikhanouskaya's Adviser: Russians, Lukashenko Regime Preparing for Escalation Along Belarus’s Borders — Frantsishak Viachorka, adviser to Belarus’s President-elect Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya, told Kyiv Post that the Lukashenko regime, with V

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ladimir Putin’s support, is preparing for offensive warfare along Belarus’s western borders, including with Ukraine, and whether escalation should be expected in the near future.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Tusk Urges NATO to Act as Medvedev Warns ‘Peaceful Sleep of EU Citizens’ Is Over — Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk has called on NATO allies to take growing Russian provocations seriously following a serious airspace violation in Romania

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

and subsequent hostile rhetoric from Moscow. The diplomatic clash was triggered when a Russian drone crashed into a residential building in the Romanian city of Galați during an attack on Ukraine, injuring a woman and a child in the first such incident to caus

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine and Canada Launch Joint Production of Reconnaissance Drones — The Ministry of Defense of Ukraine announced a joint venture with Canada to manufacture specialized reconnaissance drones for the Armed Forces of Ukraine. Under the newly

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

signed defense agreement, Ukrainian tech firm Airlogix and Canadian manufacturer Sentinel Research and Development will unite to establish a joint enterprise named Airlogix-Sentinel.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Braces for Massive Air Assault as Zelensky Warns Putin is Testing NATO Unity — Zelensky said Ukraine expects a major Russian air assault within days, citing intelligence shared by US and European partners. He urged civilians to seek

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

shelter and said Ukraine’s biggest shortage remains anti-ballistic missile interceptors.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Claims Crimea–Donbas Land Corridor Effectively Cut As Logistics Routes Come Under Fire — Ukraine’s military intelligence (HUR) on Friday said it is disrupting Russian logistics in occupied southern Ukraine by targeting the land corr

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

idor linking Crimea with Donbas. Officials claim key routes between Berdyansk, Melitopol, and Dzhankoi are under fire control, with strikes destroying fuel and transport vehicles and worsening Russia’s supply and infrastructure capacity in the occupied territo

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Confirms Massive Strikes on Russian Refineries and Command Posts — The General Staff of the Armed Forces of Ukraine has officially confirmed a series of coordinated, long-range strikes executed overnight into May 31, dealing a sever

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

e blow to Russia’s military logistics and energy networks. Beyond hitting the strategic Rosneft oil refinery in Saratov, Ukrainian forces struck a vital crude oil pumping station and a fuel depot. The operation also heavily targeted the Kremlin’s frontline cap

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Damages Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker and Feodosia Oil Terminal — The Unmanned Systems Forces (USF) of Ukraine executed a massive, coordinated deep-strike operation overnight into May 30, successfully striking 23 military targets and

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

strategic facilities deep inside Russian territory and occupied regions. Long-range drones damaged a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker and the “Kurgannefteprodukt” oil depot in Taganrog.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Issues Nationwide Missile Warning Over MiG-31K Takeoff — Ukraine issued a nationwide missile threat warning early Friday after detecting the takeoff of a Russian MiG-31K, a carrier of the Kinzhal ballistic missile. Air raid alerts w

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ere activated across all regions, and residents were urged to take shelter.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Offers Romania Drone Defense Cooperation After Russian Drone Hits Apartment Block — Ukraine is ready to deepen cooperation with Romania to counter Russian drone threats after a Russian drone struck a residential building in Galați o

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

n Friday. Foreign Minister Andriy Sybiha said the incident highlights the broader risk Russia’s war poses to the Black Sea region and Europe, while NATO and EU officials condemned it as a dangerous escalation.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Rejects Russian Accusations of Drone Strike on Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant — Ukraine’s Southern Defense Forces have dismissed Kremlin accusations regarding an alleged Ukrainian drone strike on Power Unit No. 6 of the occupied Z

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

aporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP). Ukrainian military officials emphasized that the claims are a standard Russian information provocation designed to deflect international attention from Moscow’s tactical losses. The defense forces provided concrete techn

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Scores New Battlefield Gains Near Donetsk Region Border — Ukrainian forces have regained at least 46 square kilometers of territory on the Oleksandrivka sector, military monitoring group DeepState reported. The advance follows a ser

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ies of successful Ukrainian counterattacks in 2026 that analysts have linked to intensified drone warfare and disruptions to Russian battlefield communications.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine, Russia and the Battle for Public Opinion — It’s no secret that Ukraine’s future depends very much on the degree to which avowed allies are willing to help. So keeping track of opinions about the war helps recognize both positive an

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

d negative trends. Results of recent polls show a realistic but determined Ukraine and an American public in some respects more supportive of Ukraine’s sovereignty and security than its current government, and even some European societies.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine’s Envoy Melnyk Blasts Russia’s ‘Fake’ Starobilsk Narrative at UN — Ukraine’s UN envoy Andriy Melnyk accused Russia of inventing a false victimhood narrative over the alleged Starobilsk dormitory strike, saying Moscow’s own published

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

list showed no children among the named victims. He also condemned Russia’s massive attack on Kyiv, warned Belarus against deeper involvement in the war, and urged allies to increase air-defense support tenfold.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine’s SBU Strikes Key FSB Intelligence Facility Deep Inside Russia — Ukraine’s Security Service (SBU) says its “Alpha” special forces used long-range drones to destroy a key Federal Security Service (FSB) electronic intelligence hub in

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

Russia’s Krasnodar Krai, which possibly helped guide missile, drone attacks on Ukraine.

ини нову пускову установку IRIS-T) — У суботу, 30 травня, Україна отримала від Німеччини пускову установку зенітно-ракетного комплексу IRIS-T.

льших нафтопроводів РФ із Сибіру до Європи) — Безпілотники Сил спеціальних операцій уразили потужну виробничо-диспетчерську станцію великого нафтопроводу у Кіровській області, а також нафтобазу у Ростовській області РФ у ніч на неділю.
